items.docs: Retrieves attachments matching the given request parameters. For example, this API allows you to list all attachments by investigation.
An API used to upload one or more attachments. By default, attachments are not associated with a comment. Use this API to create comment.

params.name: rrn
params.description: The RRN of the attachment.

items.docs: An API you can use to download an attachment by RRN. The RRN determines which attachment is downloaded.

params.description: The RRN of the comment.

params.name: visibility
params.description: The new visibility for the comment (case insensitive).

items.docs: An API to update visibility of a comment by using an RRN. This API returns the comment with the updated visibility .

items.docs: Retrieves a page of comments matching the given request parameters. For example, this API allows you to list all comments on an investigation by passing an investigation's RRN as the target value.

An API you can use to create comments for a particular target. The target determines where the comment will appear within InsightIDR. Only certain types of RRNs are permitted as targets, such as investigation RRNs.

params.name: rrn
params.description: Return a comment with this rrn.

items.docs: Retrieves a comment by its rrn.

params.name: rrn
params.description: The RRN of the comment.

items.docs: A delete API you can use to delete a comment by using an RRN. The RRN determines which comment will be deleted. Only the creator of a comment can delete it.

Create a private InsightIDR Community Threat and add indicators to this Community Threat. The following Community Threat indicators are supported in the request body, formatted as JSON:
  • communityThreatName
  • notetext
  • indicators
    • IP addresses (non-CIDR notation)
    • Process hashes
    • Domain names
    • URLs
The request content type must be to in JSON (see request samples).
The maximum file size is 50 MB.

info.name: Replace indicators for a Community Threat

params.name: key
params.description: The key of a Community Threat for which the indicators are going to be added.

params.name: format
params.description: The input format.

items.docs:
Replace InsightIDR Community Threat indicators for a Community Threat with the given Community Threat key. The following Community Threat indicators are supported in the request body formatted as JSON, STIX XML, or CSV:
  • IP addresses
  • Process hashes
  • Domain names
  • URLs
The request content type is expected to match the specified format: "application/json" for "json" format, "text/xml" for "stix_xml" format and "text/csv" for "csv" format. See request samp

info.name: Add indicators to a Community Threat

params.name: key
params.description: The key of a Community Threat for which the indicators are going to be added.

params.name: format
params.description: The input format.

items.docs:
Add InsightIDR Community Threat indicators to a Community Threat with the given Community Threat key. The following Community Threat indicators are supported in the request body formatted as JSON, STIX XML, or CSV:
  • IP addresses
  • Process hashes
  • Domain names
  • URLs
The request content type is expected to match the specified format: "application/json" for "json" format, "text/xml" for "stix_xml" format and "text/csv" for "csv" format. See request samples o
Delete an owned InsightIDR Community Threat.

info.name: Set the status of an investigation

params.name: id
params.description: The ID or RRN of the investigation to change the status of.

params.name: status
params.description: The new status for the investigation (case-insensitive).

items.docs: An API that sets the status of the investigation with the given ID or RRN. This API returns the investigation with its status updated to the new state. If the investigation already has the given status, no change is made, but the investigation will still be returned.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID or RRN of the investigation to assign the user to.

items.docs: An API that assigns a user to the specified investigation. The user must be a platform administrator, product administrator, or read/write user with access to either InsightIDR or InsightUBA.
An API to close all investigations that match the parameters provided in the request body. This API returns the number of investigations that were closed and a list of their IDs.

params.name: index
params.description: The optional, 0 based index of the page to retrieve.  Must be an integer greater than or equal to 0.

params.name: size
params.description: The optional size of the page to retrieve. Must be an integer greater than 0 or less then or equal to 1000.

params.name: statuses
params.description: An optional, comma-separated set of investigation statuses. Only investigations whose status matches one of the entries in the list will be returned.  If this parameter, is omitted investigations with any status may be returned.

params.name: start_time
params.description: An optional, ISO-formatted timestamp. Only investigations whose createTime is after this date will be returned by the API. If this parameter is omitted, investigations with any create_time may be returned.

params.name: end_time
params.description: An optional, ISO-formatted timestamp. Only investigations whose createTime is before this date will be returned by the API. If this parameter is omitted, investigations with any create_time may be returned.

items.docs: An API to list all investigations. This API retrieves a page of investigations matching the given request parameters. The investigations will always be sorted by investigation created_time in descending order.

params.name: index
params.description: The optional, 0 based index of the page to retrieve. Must be an integer greater than or equal to 0.

params.name: size
params.description: The optional size of the page to retrieve. Must be an integer greater than 0 or less than or equal to 1000.

params.name: resourceTypes
params.description: An optional, comma-separated set of resource types. Only resources whose type matches one of the entries in the list are returned. If this parameter is omitted, health metrics with any resource type may be returned.

params.name: orgId
params.description: Optional organization ID to get metrics for. If not specified, metrics for the user's current organization will be returned. User must have access to the specified organization.

items.docs: Retrieve paginated health metrics for the given org. In the event of a 207 response, there may be two additional metadata fields in the response. 'failed_to_load' contains the the resource types that did not successfully load. 'unlicensed_resource_types' contains resource types that were requested, but for which the org does not have access to.
Add a new collector. The API key used must be associated with a user with the collection administration role.
